purpose: Goal 1 first MBS ladder for MORI EP versus MORI EP plus native balanced-MoE compact-dispatch. This is intended to find the MBS tipping point where MindSpeed-style hot-helper should begin helping memory/load shape.
note: MBS1 results were on do-sonle5-mi350-gpu; MBS2 A/B was run as a same-node pair on do-vunguyen-mi350-gpu because do-sonle5 became occupied by an external workload. Treat cross-node MBS1-to-MBS2 scaling as directional only; the MBS2 plain-vs-balanced result is same-node comparable.

balanced_mori_compact_dispatch.read: Balanced is modestly faster in the late window but uses more peak memory. MBS1 is too small to prove the desired hot-helper memory-shape advantage.

balanced_mori_compact_dispatch.failure: MORI static heap exhaustion during dispatch/combine op construction; ranks report hip invalid argument followed by Out of heap memory requesting 402653184 bytes with 64G heap.

heap96_retry.read: Balanced compact-dispatch is faster than plain MORI at MBS2 once the heap is raised, but it still uses more peak memory and requires a larger static MORI heap. This is a throughput signal, not yet the desired hot-helper memory-shape win.

plain_mori.failure: PyTorch CUDA OOM in MORI/AITER local owner VJP SwiGLU backward: _swiglu_backward dgate allocation requested 6.00 GiB with 1.80 GiB free; 174.64 GiB allocated by PyTorch and 6.43 GiB reserved but unallocated.
plain_mori.read: Plain MORI reaches a real first optimizer step at MBS4, then hits the owner-rank VJP activation/workspace cliff in the next backward.

heap96.failure: MORI dispatch/combine op construction fails with static heap exhaustion; requested 6455033856 bytes with current heap size 103079215104 bytes.

heap128.failure: MORI dispatch/combine op construction still fails with static heap exhaustion; requested 805306368 bytes with current heap size 137438953472 bytes.

balanced_mori_compact_dispatch.read: Balanced compact-dispatch does not reach a metric step at MBS4. Increasing heap from 96G to 128G gets further but still fails in static MORI heap creation, which means current balanced compact-dispatch adds MORI heap residency instead of removing enough normal-route state.

read: The MBS ladder now identifies the first cliff. MBS1 is too small; balanced is slightly faster but higher memory. At MBS2, plain MORI fits at heap64 and balanced needs heap96, then runs about 4.8% faster but with about 13.75 GiB more peak memory. At MBS4, plain MORI reaches one step then OOMs in owner-rank VJP SwiGLU backward, while balanced compact-dispatch fails before metrics from MORI heap pressure even at heap128. So the MindSpeed-style idea is still correct, but the current native compact-dispatch implementation has not materialized the memory-shape advantage. The next source target is to make hot/cold/helper rows truly first-class in MORI dispatch/combine so balanced mode removes hot rows from the normal MORI state instead of layering extra compact-dispatch state on top.
